description: Registry of available nuclear data libraries

tendl-2023-iso.name: TENDL-2023 + Aug 2024 isomeric correction
tendl-2023-iso.description: TENDL-2023 neutron sublibrary with August 2024 patch restoring isomeric production in MF-10 (ground/metastable state splitting for ~15k products). No neutron sublibrary. Use alongside tendl-2025 for isomeric ratios not available in the newer release.
tendl-2023-iso.note: Previously mislabeled as tendl-2024. This is NOT an official TENDL release — it is the TENDL-2023 charged-particle sublibrary with the Aug 2024 isomeric correction applied. States: '' (total), 'g' (ground), 'm' (metastable). TENDL-2025 dropped the g/m split entirely.

jeff-4.0.name: JEFF-4.0
jeff-4.0.description: Joint Evaluated Fission and Fusion File (NEA)

irdff-2.name: IRDFF-II
irdff-2.description: International Reactor Dosimetry and Fusion File (IAEA)

hi-xs-prod.name: HI-XS Production (Geant4 INCL++/ABLA07)
hi-xs-prod.description: Heavy-ion production cross-sections σ(Zf,Af,E) computed via Geant4 11.3.2 FTFP_INCLXX physics list (INCL++ cascade + ABLA07 de-excitation).  Covers 6 projectiles × 92 targets × ~60 energy points (1–1000 MeV/u).  Normalized to Tripathi (1997) total reaction XS.

hi-xs.name: HI-XS (Tripathi 1997)
hi-xs.description: Heavy-ion total reaction cross-sections computed with the Tripathi (1997) semi-empirical parameterization.  Covers all projectile/target combinations Z=1-92.

strata-data-nuclear.name: Geant4-derived nuclear structure + EM (via strata-data)
strata-data-nuclear.description: G4ENSDFSTATE3.0 + PhotonEvaporation6.1.2 + RadioactiveDecay6.1.2 republished as Parquet by the strata project — source for nuclides.parquet / ground_states.parquet / levels/ / radiation/ / decay.parquet from v0.11.0 onward (replaces the v0.10.x IAEA-LiveChart pipeline). v0.13+ also pulls the em/ subdir for ESTAR + Sternheimer density-effect data (issue #121). See ADR-0002.

meta.spectrum_xs_note: Spectrum-averaged neutron XS for thermal (Maxwellian kT=0.0253eV), epithermal (1/E), and fast (Watt fission) spectra. Built by nucl_parquet.build_spectrum_xs.
meta.compound_compositions_note: Elemental compositions (Z, weight_fraction) for the 33 NIST XCOM standard materials (water, air, tissue, bone, plastics, glasses, …). One row per (material, Z); weight fractions sum to 1.0 ± 1e-6 per material. Source: NIST XCOM Table 4 + ICRU-44. Join on `material` to xcom_compounds (integrated µ/ρ) or to photon_pe_total/photon_compton/photon_pair for Bragg-additive per-process σ. Built by nucl_parquet.build_compound_compositions.
meta.nudex_shellcor_note: Microscopic shell corrections + ground-state β₂/β₄ deformation parameters per (Z, A) from strata's NUDEX nudex_shellcor_ms. Schema: Z/A/symbol/shell_MeV/deformation_correction_MeV/beta2/beta4. Used by Hauser-Feshbach codes. Closes #77.
meta.nudex_runtime_config_note: nudex_special_inputs and nudex_general_stat are NUDEX runtime configuration tables (per-isotope overrides + defaults) shipped for audit/completeness — not load-bearing for typical consumers.

stopping.layout: one file per source — stopping/{source}.parquet (e.g. PSTAR.parquet, catima_C12.parquet)
stopping.sources_note: PSTAR/ASTAR/ESTAR are NIST ICRU-49/37 tables (reproducible via nucl_parquet.build_stopping). dSTAR/tSTAR are velocity-scaled from PSTAR (exact for electronic stopping). ³He has no NIST table — routes through catima in the loader. catima_* are pycatima tables for common heavy-ion beams; the full 92×92 MeV/u matrix is at catima_full_matrix.

catima.isotope_inventory: All stable + primordial isotopes (abundance > 0 in meta/abundances.parquet, 287 nuclides) ∪ all ground-state nuclides with T½ > 1 year in meta/decay.parquet (covers Np/Pu/Am/Cm/Bk/Cf/Es transuranics up to Z=99, plus every long-lived radio-isotope of lower-Z elements) ∪ beam/medical allowlist (C-11, N-13, O-15, F-18, P-32). For Z values with no T½>1yr isotope (At, Rn, Fr), the longest-lived ground state is shipped as fallback.
catima.isotope_note: Rows are keyed on (proj_Z, proj_A). At E/A > 0.1 MeV/u dedx is isotope-agnostic to <0.1%; below ~0.01 MeV/u nuclear-stopping reduced-mass effects cause real (up to ~15%) isotope dependence, captured by the per-isotope rows. Querying (Z, A) outside the inventory raises KeyError — extend nucl_parquet.build_heavy_ions._BEAM_ALLOWLIST or lower _LONGLIVED_THRESHOLD_S and rebuild.

electron_stopping.coverage: 98 elements (Z=1..98) + ~183 compounds (ICRU tissue/detector materials, water, air, etc.)
electron_stopping.note: target_Z is NULL for compounds; query elements by target_Z, compounds by name. Both collision + radiative + total stopping power columns are populated.

density_effect_params.coverage: 278 materials covering the standard Geant4 NIST_MATERIAL set (elements with G4_ prefix, plus ICRU compounds)
density_effect_params.note: Names use the G4_<symbol>/G4_<COMPOUND> convention from PhysicsList. For elements Zeff equals Z. Use these params to evaluate the density-effect correction δ above the validity range of tabulated dedx.

compounds.note: NIST-measured proton and α stopping-power tables for the standard ICRU-37 compound set (water liquid + vapor, A-150 plastic, polyethylene, tissue/bone substitutes, air, …). Direct drop-in replacement for naive Bragg additivity (Σ wᵢ · elemental_dedx) which is 5–10% off near the Bragg peak for these compounds. `matno` is NIST's compound identifier preserved for audit; `compound` is NIST verbatim normalized to UPPER_SNAKE_CASE.

summing_partners.layout: one file per daughter element symbol — meta/ensdf/summing_partners/{Symbol}.parquet
summing_partners.source: Derived from coincidences/{Symbol}.parquet with ICC correction factors. γ-γ pairs canonicalized (E1 ≤ E2), X-ray/Auger ⊗ γ pairs from mixed_coincidences.
summing_partners.coverage: 104 elements, ~9.3M rows. All ground + metastable parent decay channels.
summing_partners.note: ICC-corrected summing partner pairs for HPGe true-coincidence-summing (TCS) corrections. icc_correction_factor = 1/((1+α₁)×(1+α₂)) for γ-γ; 1/(1+α₂) for xray⊗γ. pure_emission_joint_intensity = pair_intensity × icc_correction_factor. Filed by daughter element (Co-60 cascades under Ni). Validated: Eu-152 344 keV ≥10 partners, Co-60 1173/1333 ICC ≈ 0.9997.

emissions.layout: one file per parent element symbol — meta/ensdf/emissions/{Symbol}.parquet
emissions.source: Cascade propagation through G4 PhotonEvaporation level schemes, fed by decay_detailed.parquet branching ratios. X-ray/Auger from radiation/ (xray_auger.py). β/α endpoints from decay_detailed Q-values.
emissions.coverage: All ground + metastable parents with known decays, Z=1..117. ~711k rows across 117 elements.
emissions.note: NuDat-equivalent one-stop-shop: absolute per-decay emission intensities (0–100%) for gamma, CE, X-ray, Auger, 511 keV annihilation, β⁻/β⁺ endpoints, and α kinetic energies. Filed by PARENT element (Co-60 in Co.parquet). Key physics: G4 intensity is photon-only relative; total transition rate = intensity × (1+ICC). Validated against NuDat: Co-60 1173 keV ±0.01%, Tc-99m 140 keV ±0.02%, Eu-152 121.8 keV ±0.3%, Na-22 511 keV ±0.07%, Ra-226 α 4784 keV.

beta_spectra.layout: one file per parent element symbol — meta/ensdf/beta_spectra/{Symbol}.parquet
beta_spectra.source: Endpoint Q-values + forbiddenness from strata radioactive_decay.parquet; spectrum shape from Fermi function (relativistic, with shape factors for unique 1st/2nd forbidden)
beta_spectra.coverage: All β-minus and β+ transitions with q_value > 0, parent Z=1..99; ~23,500 transitions × 200 energy bins each
beta_spectra.note: dN/dE is normalized per transition: ∫ dN/dE · dE = 1. `cumulative` is the CDF (useful for inverse-CDF sampling). Non-unique forbidden transitions (K-40, Tc-99, Re-187, Cl-36, etc.) use S(E)=1 as a documented approximation — filter on shape_factor_approx=True to find them. For β+ transitions, endpoint_keV is the positron kinetic-energy endpoint (strata's q_value_kev minus 2·m_e c² = 1022 keV). State 'm' marks metastable parents (inferred from parent_ex_kev > 0). Validated: P-32 mean E = 694.9 (NNDC 694.9, exact); F-18 β+ mean E = 250 keV (NNDC 249.8); Bi-210 mean E = 390 (NNDC 389).

electron_brem_sb_dcs.coverage: Z=1..92, 57 incident-electron energies (1 keV..10 GeV log-spaced), 32 κ values
electron_brem_sb_dcs.note: SCALED differential cross section per Berger & Seltzer (1985): dcs = (dσ/dκ) × β² / Z², where κ = E_γ / T_electron and β is the electron velocity. The Z² is factored out so values are O(1-10) across all Z. To recover the raw dσ/dκ multiply by Z²/β². Companion to electron_brem (integrated σ). Cite Seltzer & Berger, Nucl. Instrum. Meth. B12 (1985) 95.
